engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda E-Series Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Mazda E-Series in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK commercial market data.

cards.altText: Mazda E-Series RF engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK commercial
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - belt snaps between 60k-90k miles if not replaced, causing valve damage.
Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade on high-mileage delivery vans, causing rough idle.
Head gasket leak - coolant-oil mixing on pre-1995 units under heavy commercial load.

cards.altText: Mazda E-Series WL engine - diesel 2.5L replacement UK commercial
knownFailures: Turbo failure - turbocharger seal wear causes oil consumption and boost loss on high-mileage units.
Injector pump wear - mechanical injection pumps lose calibration after 100k miles on fleet vehicles.
Oil leak at camshaft seal - persistent leaks common on vans doing short urban delivery cycles.

cards.altText: Mazda E-Series FE F2 engine - petrol diesel 2.0L 2.2L replacement UK commercial
knownFailures: Head gasket weakness - FE petrol engines prone to gasket failure under commercial load if overheated.
Timing chain tensioner wear - F2 diesel units develop rattles after 80k miles if service intervals missed.
Carburettor/fuel pump issues - early FE petrol units suffer fuel delivery problems on stop-start commercial use.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £600-£1,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable RF/WL units - actual quotes may vary for early F2/FE codes.

engineGuide.h2: Mazda E-Series Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mazda E-Series across all UK generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK commercial specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The RF 2.0L diesel powered most early E-Series commercial vans; reliable but timing belt critical.
compatibleVariants: Van, Pickup, Chassis Cab, Dropside, SWB, LWB
commonFailures: Timing belt failure at 60k-90k miles if not replaced - causes valve damage
Injector wear on high-mileage delivery vans causing rough idle and misfires
Head gasket leak on pre-1995 units under heavy commercial load
entries.cta: Get quotes for RF Mazda E-Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: Turbocharged RF variant for higher payload applications; boost control requires attention.
commonFailures: Turbocharger seal wear causing oil consumption and boost loss
Boost control solenoid failure leading to reduced power under load
Intercooler pipe leaks on high-mileage commercial vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for RF-T Mazda E-Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The higher-output WL replaced RF in many applications; more power but turbo/injector complexity.
compatibleVariants: Pickup, Van, Chassis Cab, Dropside, Tipper, 4WD, LWB
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure between 80k-120k miles on fleet vehicles
Mechanical injector pump wear causing hard starting and power loss
Oil leak at camshaft seal on short-trip urban delivery vans
entries.cta: Get quotes for WL Mazda E-Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: Top-spec turbo WL for heavy-duty commercial use; requires regular turbo maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Pickup, Tipper, Chassis Cab, 4WD, high-payload Van
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal failure causing blue smoke and oil consumption
Injector nozzle wear leading to poor fuel economy and rough running
EGR valve clogging on stop-start commercial duty cycles
entries.cta: Get quotes for WL-T Mazda E-Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Early-generation diesel for launch-year E-Series; simpler but parts increasingly scarce.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles after 80k miles
Mechanical injector pump calibration drift on high-mileage units
Glow plug relay failure causing cold-start difficulties

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Budget petrol option for light commercial use; less common but cost-effective to rebuild.
compatibleVariants: Van, Chassis Cab (base trim, early models)
commonFailures: Head gasket weakness under commercial load if overheated
Carburettor/fuel pump issues on stop-start urban delivery use
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles on neglected units

commonProblems.h2: Mazda E-Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda E-Series commercial range is generally robust but high-mileage fleet use exposes specific weaknesses in RF timing belts and WL turbo systems. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 6‑month unlimited mileage trade warranty. Use the problem groups below as a decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mazda E-Series RF Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mazda E-Series Van, Pickup, Chassis Cab (1984-1999, RF & RF-T 2.0L diesel)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, valves collide with pistons causing catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys and water pump before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years on commercial vehicles

repairOptions.tier: Major - Valve Repair After Belt Snap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement/regrind, head gasket, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block/pistons undamaged; often not cost-effective on older commercial vans

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt RF engine with new timing kit, upgraded gaskets, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial vehicles; includes 6-month trade warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Mazda E-Series Van worth £1,500-£4,000, preventative timing belt replacement is excellent value. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt RF engine often provides better fleet ROI than major valve repairs, especially with our 6-month unlimited mileage trade warranty.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RF engine prices for your Mazda E-Series

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why WL Diesel Pickups Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mazda E-Series Pickup, Tipper, Chassis Cab (1988-1999, WL-T & WL 2.5L turbo diesel)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil seals wear from heat cycles and infrequent oil changes; oil leaks into intake or exhaust, causing smoke, boost loss, and eventual turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo seals, bearings, clean housing; requires skilled technician
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo housing undamaged; not recommended if shaft play or wheel damage present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit refurbished or new turbo unit, replace oil feed/return lines, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine otherwise healthy; ensure oil system clean to protect new turbo

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt WL Engine with Tested Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt WL engine with tested/refurbished turbo, renewed injectors, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vehicles; addresses turbo AND injector wear in one solution

problems.recommendation: If your WL-T Pickup has over 100k miles and shows turbo symptoms, replacing just the turbo may be a short-term fix. For fleet vehicles, a rebuilt WL engine with tested turbo and renewed injectors often delivers better long-term uptime and lower cost-per-mile.
problems.cta: Get quotes for WL-T Mazda E-Series engine replacement

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Rough Running on High-Mileage E-Series Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Mazda E-Series Van, Pickup, Chassis Cab (1984-1999, RF & WL diesel families)
problems.rootCause: Mechanical injector nozzles wear from fuel contamination and high-pressure cycles; causes poor atomisation, rough idle, black smoke, and reduced fuel economy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test, and replace worn injector nozzles; recalibrate pump if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage injector wear; requires skilled diesel technician

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild or replace mechanical injection pump, renew all injectors, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine compression healthy; ensures precise fuel delivery for commercial duty

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Renewed Injection System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt RF/WL engine with tested/renewed injectors and pump, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for vehicles over 100k miles; addresses injector wear AND other age-related failures

problems.recommendation: For E-Series commercial vans under 80k miles, injector service is cost-effective. For fleet vehicles approaching 100k+ miles, a rebuilt engine with renewed injection system often provides better ROI and reduces risk of secondary failures.
problems.cta: Compare diesel injector replacement vs rebuilt engine costs for your Mazda E-Series

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Overheating on Commercial RF/FE Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mazda E-Series Van, Chassis Cab (1984-1995, RF & FE 2.0L engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-110,000 miles, especially on vehicles with cooling system neglect
problems.rootCause: Original head gasket material degrades under thermal stress; coolant leaks into cylinders or oil passages, causing overheating, white smoke, or coolant-oil mixing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if head/block undamaged; risk of repeat failure if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head pressure test, valve grind, multi-layer steel gasket upgrade, full reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity than basic gasket swap; recommended for commercial vehicles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt RF/FE engine with upgraded MLS gasket, renewed water pump, full cooling system check
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vehicles; addresses gasket AND other age-related wear in one solution

problems.recommendation: If your E-Series Van shows early head gasket symptoms (coolant loss, overheating), act quickly. For vehicles over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket often provides better fleet ROI than repeated head repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RF/FE rebuilt engines with upgraded gaskets for your Mazda E-Series

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda E-Series Commercial Vehicle
finalCta.paragraph: Timing belt snaps, turbo failures, injector wear, and head gasket leaks are common on high-mileage E-Series vans and pickups. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK commercial engine specialists who understand fleet uptime needs. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a 6-month unlimited mileage trade warranty and nationwide supply & fit options. Compare prices today and keep your commercial vehicle on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda E-Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK commercial network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda E-Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 6‑month unlimited mileage trade warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mazda E-Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mazda E-Series commercial range is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Mazda E-Series 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L RF diesel was the backbone of the early E-Series commercial range (1984-1999), powering most Van, Pickup and Chassis Cab variants. Naturally aspirated and turbocharged versions offered a balance of fuel economy and payload capability for light commercial duty.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure at 60k-90k miles; injector wear on high-mileage units; head gasket leaks on pre-1995 engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda E-Series 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda E-Series 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L F2 diesel powered launch-year E-Series models (1984-1990) before the RF became standard. Simpler mechanical injection but parts increasingly scarce; verify chassis year before ordering replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear; mechanical injector pump calibration drift; glow plug relay failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda E-Series 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda E-Series 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L WL diesel (1988-1999) offered higher power for heavy-duty Pickup, Tipper and Chassis Cab applications. Turbocharged WL-T variants delivered peak torque for laden commercial use but require regular turbo maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger seal wear at 80k-120k miles; injector pump wear on fleet vehicles; oil leaks at camshaft seal
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda E-Series 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda E-Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L FE petrol engine was a budget option for light commercial E-Series Van and Chassis Cab models (1984-1995). Less common than diesel but cost-effective to rebuild; verify fuel type before ordering replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket weakness under commercial load; carburettor/fuel pump issues on stop-start use; timing chain tensioner wear
items.cta: Compare Mazda E-Series 2.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Mazda E-Series Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mazda E-Series engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK commercial supply options - from early FE petrol to the later WL turbo diesel.

items.title: Mazda E-Series Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power dominated the E-Series commercial range (1984-1999), with RF 2.0L and WL 2.5L families covering most van, pickup and chassis cab applications across UK fleet operations.
items.descriptor: Diesel power dominated the E-Series commercial range (1984-1999), with RF 2.0L and WL 2.5L families covering most van, pickup and chassis cab applications across UK fleet operations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mazda E-Series Diesel Engine Families
families: RF - most common replacement engine (Van/Pickup/Chassis Cab), timing belt critical
WL - higher-output option for heavy-duty Pickup/Tipper, turbo maintenance essential
F2 - early-generation diesel (1984-1990), simpler but parts increasingly scarce
foundIn: E-Series Chassis Cab (1984-1999) - RF, WL, FE
knownFor: Strong fuel economy for light commercial duty cycles
RF timing belt failures between 60k-90k miles if not replaced
WL turbo wear on high-mileage fleet vehicles
High UK replacement demand on delivery vans and pickups
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda E-Series Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mazda E-Series Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol FE engines offered a budget option for light commercial E-Series Van and Chassis Cab models (1984-1995), though diesel variants dominate UK replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Petrol FE engines offered a budget option for light commercial E-Series Van and Chassis Cab models (1984-1995), though diesel variants dominate UK replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mazda E-Series Petrol Engine Families
families: FE - 2.0L petrol for base-trim Van/Chassis Cab, cost-effective rebuilds
foundIn: E-Series Van (1984-1995) - FE 2.0L petrol, base trim
E-Series Chassis Cab (1984-1995) - FE 2.0L petrol, early models
knownFor: Lower upfront cost than diesel variants
Head gasket weakness under heavy commercial load if overheated
Less common in UK commercial parc; parts availability more limited

engineYears.h2: Mazda E-Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mazda E-Series commercial production timeline (1984-1999), organised so fleet managers can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original E-Series launched with F2 2.2L diesel and FE 2.0L petrol engines for Van, Pickup and Chassis Cab applications.
keyChanges: Launch of Mazda E-Series commercial range in UK market
F2 2.2L diesel introduced for Van and Pickup variants
FE 2.0L petrol offered as budget option for light commercial duty
Early chassis design with simple mechanical injection systems
mainEngines: F2 - 2.2L diesel (Van / Pickup / Chassis Cab, early models)
FE - 2.0L petrol (Van / Chassis Cab, base trim)
knownFor: Simple, robust mechanical design ideal for early commercial use
Timing chain tensioner wear on neglected F2 units
FE head gasket weakness under heavy load if overheated
Parts availability becoming limited for launch-year engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-1987 Mazda E-Series replacement engines

years.description: The RF 2.0L diesel became the new standard, while the higher-output WL 2.5L arrived for heavy-duty Pickup and Chassis Cab applications.
keyChanges: RF 2.0L diesel introduced, replacing F2 as standard commercial engine
WL 2.5L diesel launched for higher-payload Pickup and Chassis Cab variants
Turbocharged RF-T and WL-T options added for demanding commercial duty
Dropside and Tipper body styles introduced with WL power
mainEngines: RF - 2.0L diesel (Van / Pickup / Chassis Cab / Dropside)
knownFor: RF timing belt critical - failure causes catastrophic valve damage
WL turbo reliability excellent with regular oil changes
Injector wear common on high-mileage delivery vans
Strong parts availability for RF/WL families in UK commercial network
years.cta: Get quotes for 1988-1993 Mazda E-Series replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda E-Series RF timing belt replacement • Mazda E-Series WL turbo failure fix • Mazda E-Series diesel injector replacement commercial
enquiries: Mazda E-Series RF timing belt replacement
Mazda E-Series WL turbo failure fix
Mazda E-Series diesel injector replacement commercial

years.preview: Final Production: RF/WL Maturity & Efficiency Updates
years.description: The final E-Series years saw refined RF and WL engines with efficiency-focused variants (RF-VE, WL-HE) and improved injector specifications for emissions compliance.
keyChanges: Efficiency-focused RF-VE and WL-HE variants introduced with updated injectors
Final production year 1999 with most common RF/WL engine specifications
Improved cooling system components for commercial duty reliability
Last UK-supplied E-Series commercial vehicles before model discontinuation
mainEngines: RF / RF-VE / RF-HE - 2.0L diesel (all commercial body styles)
WL / WL-VE / WL-HE - 2.5L diesel (Pickup / Tipper / Chassis Cab / 4WD)
RF-T / WL-T - turbo variants for high-payload applications
knownFor: Most reliable E-Series engines with updated components
Timing belt still critical on RF family - replace every 60k miles
WL turbo seals benefit from synthetic oil and regular changes
Best availability for rebuilt units from UK commercial breakers
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1999 Mazda E-Series replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda E-Series 1999 engine replacement • Mazda E-Series WL-HE injector upgrade • Mazda E-Series RF-VE commercial rebuild

faq.h2: Mazda E-Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mazda E-Series engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Mazda E-Series engine costs range from £600 (used commercial) to £2,200 (reconditioned trade) including VAT and commercial warranty, depending on engine code, condition and supplier.
keyPoints: RF 2.0L diesel rebuilt: £600-£1,800 supply only
WL 2.5L turbo rebuilt: £800-£2,200 supply only
Used engines from £600 with 3-6 month trade warranty
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on location
items.cta: Compare Mazda E-Series engine prices from UK commercial specialists

items.question: What engine code is in my Mazda E-Series?
items.answer: Find your Mazda E-Series engine code stamped on the engine block near the gearbox or on the commercial vehicle under-bonnet sticker; RF and WL are most common.
keyPoints: RF codes: RF, RF-T, RF-VE, RF-HE (2.0L diesel)
WL codes: WL, WL-T, WL-VE, WL-HE (2.5L diesel)
Early models: F2 (2.2L diesel) or FE (2.0L petrol)
Enter commercial reg for instant DVLA verification
items.cta: Verify your Mazda E-Series engine code instantly

items.question: Is a Mazda E-Series commercial engine worth replacing?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 50% of commercial vehicle value (£1,500-£5,000 typical), a reconditioned engine often provides better fleet ROI than scrappage or expensive dealer repairs.
keyPoints: Rebuilt RF/WL engines cost £600-£2,200 vs £3k+ dealer repairs
6-month unlimited mileage trade warranty protects investment
Minimal downtime with approved commercial fitting network
Extend vehicle life vs writing off viable commercial asset
items.cta: Calculate ROI for your Mazda E-Series engine replacement

items.question: How long does Mazda E-Series engine replacement take?
items.answer: Standard Mazda E-Series engine replacement takes 1-2 days including diagnostics and testing at approved commercial vehicle garages with next-day engine collection available.
keyPoints: Next-day engine collection available for orders before 12pm
Approved commercial specialists experienced with E-Series fitting
Coordinated logistics minimise fleet vehicle downtime
Supply & fit packages include diagnostics and post-fit testing
items.cta: Book your Mazda E-Series engine supply & fit appointment

items.question: What warranty comes with Mazda E-Series replacement engines?
items.answer: All Mazda E-Series reconditioned engines include a 6-month unlimited mileage trade warranty covering internal components for commercial use, with extended options available.
keyPoints: 6-month unlimited mileage trade warranty standard on rebuilt units
Coverage includes internal components, gaskets and seals
Warranty valid at any UK commercial vehicle specialist
Extended warranty options available for fleet contracts
items.cta: View Mazda E-Series engine warranty terms and coverage

items.question: Why did my Mazda E-Series diesel engine fail?
items.answer: RF timing failures often stem from missed servicing on high-mileage vans - our rebuilds include new belt, tensioners and water pump; WL turbo wear from infrequent oil changes.
keyPoints: RF timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles or 5 years
WL turbo seals wear from heat cycles and oil neglect
Injector wear common on stop-start commercial delivery cycles
Rebuilt engines address root causes with upgraded components
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda E-Series engine with failure-point upgrades

items.question: How do I know if my Mazda E-Series needs a new engine?
items.answer: Persistent knocking, loss of compression, excessive oil consumption or turbo boost loss typically indicate need for commercial replacement engine after professional diagnosis.
keyPoints: Compression test recommended before committing to replacement
Blue/white smoke often indicates internal wear requiring rebuild
Loss of power under load may be turbo or injector related
Get professional diagnosis from approved commercial specialist first
items.cta: Find a Mazda E-Series engine diagnostic specialist near you

items.question: Are used Mazda E-Series engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Mazda E-Series engines from reputable UK commercial breakers can offer good value with 3-6 month warranty - recommend verification of mileage and compression test results before purchase.
keyPoints: Source from breakers specialising in commercial vehicles
Request compression test results and service history where available
Budget option best for older vehicles with limited residual value
Rebuilt units offer better longevity for critical fleet applications
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Mazda E-Series engine options

items.question: Where can I get my Mazda E-Series engine fitted?
items.answer: Enter your postcode to find approved Mazda E-Series engine fitting specialists for commercial vehicles in your area, with supply & fit packages available nationwide.
keyPoints: Network of 100+ UK commercial vehicle specialists
Experienced with E-Series van, pickup and chassis cab installations
Coordinated logistics for minimal fleet downtime
Single-point accountability for engine and fitting warranty
items.cta: Find Mazda E-Series engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mazda E-Series commercial vehicle?
items.answer: Engine swaps on commercial vehicles require ECU reprogramming and mounting verification; we recommend supply of OEM-spec replacement matching original engine code for reliability and compliance.
keyPoints: RF and WL families generally interchangeable within chassis type
Turbo/non-turbo swaps require ECU and wiring modifications
Verify PTO compatibility for Chassis Cab and Tipper applications
OEM-spec replacement ensures emissions and insurance compliance
items.cta: Get quotes for OEM-spec Mazda E-Series engine replacement
